classes.name: BundlesService
classes.description:
Defines a BundlesService, whice is the main entry point for Sijil operations.

A BundlesServices has three dependencies :

  • RequireService : Used to fetch bundles
  • Parser : Used to parse logic inside bundle values
  • SijilOpts : An object containing custom settings
classes.rawdescription:
Defines a BundlesService, whice is the main entry point for Sijil operations.

A BundlesServices has three dependencies :
- RequireService : Used to fetch bundles
- Parser : Used to parse logic inside bundle values
- SijilOpts : An object containing custom settings

methods.name: addToBundle
methods.rawdescription: Add keys/values to an existing bundle, or create it if missing.
methods.description: Add keys/values to an existing bundle, or create it if missing.

jsdoctags.comment: An object containing translations as key / values.
The language to map the bundle with, or the current langugage if omitted.
Returns a list of all loaded languages.

methods.name: loadBundle
methods.rawdescription:
Loads a bundle and associates it with a language.
If the target language already contains key/values, then we mixin the new bundle and the existing one.
methods.description: Loads a bundle and associates it with a language. If the target language already contains key/values, then we mixin the new bundle and the existing one.

jsdoctags.comment: The path, or whatever the RequireService needs to fetch the bundle.
The target language, or the current language if omitted.
A Promise, because the RequireService can be (is - by default) asynchronous.

methods.name: loadBundles
methods.rawdescription:
Loads multiple bundles and associates then with a language.

See {@link loadBundle}
methods.description:
Loads multiple bundles and associates then with a language.

See {@link loadBundle}

methods.name: translate
methods.rawdescription: Translates a single key into a target language, using the parameters provided if needed.
methods.description: Translates a single key into a target language, using the parameters provided if needed.

jsdoctags.comment: Parameters to use if the translation contains logic.
Target language, of the current language if omitted.
The computed translation, or the key if no match was found in the bundles (including the fallback language bundle).

methods.name: unloadBundle

classes.name: FragmentsParser
classes.description:
The default Sijil parser.

  • Input : bundle value (translation) + parameters (Object or Array)
  • Output : compiled translation

Logic is contained inside mustache blocks : {{ LOGIC BLOCK }}

There are two variants :

  • A single parameter key (when the params are contained inside an object) or index (params contained inside an array)

Examples:

{{ key }} + { "key" : "my key" } = my key

{{ 1 }} + [1, 2] = 2

  • A ternary-like condition

{{ condition ? trueValue : falseValue }} Where condition may be : a single parameter key/index, or 2 clauses with the following operators : ==, >, =>, <=, <

Examples:

(the $ sign to refer to a variable is mandatory when a clause contains more than 1 word)

{{ count > 1 ? $count cats : 1 cat }} + {"count": 10} = 10 cats {{ 1 < count ? $count cats : 1 cat }} + {"count": 1} = 1 cat
classes.rawdescription:
The default Sijil parser.

- Input : bundle value (translation) + parameters (Object or Array)
- Output : compiled translation

Logic is contained inside mustache blocks : {{ LOGIC BLOCK }}

There are two variants :
 - A single parameter key (when the params are contained inside an object) or index (params contained inside an array)

Examples:

{{ key }} + { "key" : "my key" } = my key

{{ 1 }} + [1, 2] = 2

 - A ternary-like condition

{{ condition ? trueValue : falseValue }}
Where condition may be : a single parameter key/index, or 2 clauses with the following operators : ==, >, =>, <=, <

Examples:

(the $ sign to refer to a variable is mandatory when a clause contains more than 1 word)

{{ count > 1 ? $count cats : 1 cat }} + {"count": 10} = 10 cats
{{ 1 < count ? $count cats : 1 cat }} + {"count": 1} = 1 cat

components.name: OdeComponent
PageComponent
components.template:
Pages in Storybook

We recommend building UIs with a component-driven process starting with atomic components and ending with pages.

Render pages with mock data. This makes it easy to build and review page states without needing to navigate to them in your app. Here are some handy patterns for managing page data in Storybook:

  • Use a higher-level connected component. Storybook helps you compose such data from the "args" of child component stories
  • Assemble data in the page component from your services. You can mock these services out using Storybook.

Get a guided tutorial on component-driven development at Storybook tutorials . Read more in the docs .

Tip Adjust the width of the canvas with the Viewports addon in the toolbar
components.sourceCode:
import { Component } from "@angular/core"; import { User } from "./User"; @Component({ selector: "storybook-page", template: `

Pages in Storybook

We recommend building UIs with a component-driven process starting with atomic components and ending with pages.

Render pages with mock data. This makes it easy to build and review page states without needing to navigate to them in your app. Here are some handy patterns for managing page data in Storybook:

  • Use a higher-level connected component. Storybook helps you compose such data from the "args" of child component stories
  • Assemble data in the page component from your services. You can mock these services out using Storybook.

Get a guided tutorial on component-driven development at Storybook tutorials . Read more in the docs .

Tip Adjust the width of the canvas with the Viewports addon in the toolbar
`, styleUrls: ["./page.css"], }) export default class PageComponent { user: User | null = null; doLogout() { this.user = null; } doLogin() { this.user = { name: "Jane Doe" }; } doCreateAccount() { this.user = { name: "Jane Doe" }; } }

typealiases.name: OnCheckData
typealiases.description:
ListCheckableComponent is an alternative to the ListComponent to easily select rows by using a checkbox.

``` <ode-list-checkable [readOnly]="a_boolean" [model]="an_array_of_items" [filters]="" [sort]="'+displayName'" [limit]="a_number" [noResultsLabel]="text_to_display_when_no_item_in_model" [isChecked]="a_lambda_which_takes_1_item_argument_and_returns_a_boolean" [areAllChecked]="a_lambda_with_no_argument_which_returns_a_boolean" [ngClass]="a_lambda_which_takes_1_item_argument_and_returns_an_object" (onCheck)="emits_an_$event_like_{item:any; checked:boolean}" (listChange)="" (scrolledDown)=""

{{item.displayName|translate}} `̀``
